analysis_description: Round 4 Phase 1 (suggested direction.txt): merged trades with same-timestamp price mids; forward mid change = K-th subsequent price row for (day, product) after trade ts; aggression via price vs BBO; burst calendar; buyer→seller edge graph; participant-level mean forward by (U, role, product, K) pooled and by day; (buyer,seller,symbol) baseline mean fwd20 residuals; burst vs random control forward extract 20 rows.
used_to_decide: Phase 1 completion gate embedded in round4_phase1_complete below; proceed to Phase 2 only after review.

bullets.conclusion: Pooled aggressive buys by Mark 67 on VELVETFRUIT_EXTRACT show positive mean forward mid at K=5,20,100 (n~164, t>1.8); Mark 55 aggressive buy on extract positive at K=5,20 (n~598); Mark 22 aggressive sell on VEV_5300 negative mean at K=5 (n=160, t~-3.3). By-day splits show Mark 67 extract signal present days 1–2 but reverses day 3 at K=100 — not fully stable across all three days. Spread-quantile stratification: r4_participant_forward_by_spreadq.csv.

bullets.bullet: 2) Deviation from bot baseline (buyer,seller,symbol cell means)
bullets.conclusion: Residuals vs (buyer,seller,product) mean forward-20 computed; top pairs dominated by high-count Mark 01→22 / hydro duopoly cells — inspect CSV for second-order structure; no single residual rule promoted to trade without further shrinkage.
Mark 01→Mark 22 dominates count (1339 rows); Mark 14↔Mark 38 hydro reciprocity matches round4work initial summary — structural basket/duopoly roles confirmed.

Sampled 200 burst timestamps vs 600 random extract timestamps: mean forward U mid +20 rows is +0.22 after bursts vs -0.07 on random controls — weak positive event-study hint, not yet sized for execution.
Mark 55 aggressive sell on extract shows negative mean forward at K=100 (n=595, t~-2.5) — population-level adverse selection proxy for liquidity takers selling extract; no full-book inventory model.

top5_tradeable_edges.edge: After Mark 67 aggressive buy on VELVETFRUIT_EXTRACT, pooled forward mid rises (K=5 mean ~1.96, K=20 ~1.81; n=164)
top5_tradeable_edges.effect_size: K=5 mean +1.96 mid units; t~13 pooled
top5_tradeable_edges.days_validated: Strong days 1–2; K=100 weak on day 3 — partial stability

top5_tradeable_edges.edge: Mark 55 aggressive buy on VELVETFRUIT_EXTRACT — positive forward at K=5,20 (n~598)
top5_tradeable_edges.days_validated: Pooled 3 days; split by day in by_day CSV

top5_tradeable_edges.edge: Mark 22 aggressive sell on VEV_5300 — negative short-horizon forward mid (K=5 mean ~-0.20, n=160)
top5_tradeable_edges.days_validated: Pooled; aligns with dedicated 01 buy / 22 sell pipe in initial summary

top5_tradeable_edges.edge: Multi-print burst timestamps — mildly higher forward extract mid (+20 rows) vs random time control
Mark 55 aggressive sell extract — negative long-horizon forward (K=100)
top5_tradeable_edges.days_validated: Candidate fade / avoid providing liquidity at ask after aggressive sell

no_edge_findings: Many (U, product, K) cells have n<30 or |t|<1.5 after aggression filter — no claim.
Mark 67 extract long-horizon (K=100) sign unstable on day 3 — do not trade K=100 off Mark 67 alone without regime split.
Residual baseline table alone does not yet yield a simple fade rule without overfit check on holdout day.
round4_phase1_complete.criteria_note: Horizon K = next K rows in prices CSV for same (day, product) sorted by timestamp (documented in analyze_r4_phase1_counterparty.py).

analysis_description: Round 4 Phase 2: analyze_r4_phase2.py — burst-window (±300 ts) around Mark01→Mark22 multi-VEV centers; microprice minus mid in px table; spread first-diff vs |fwd_5|; extract signed-flow lags OLS vs fwd_5; Mark22 on VEV_5300 fwd_5 by spread bucket.
used_to_decide: Phase 2 completion gate in round4_phase2_complete; trader_v1 tests Mark22-on-5300 fade (negative sim PnL overall — falsifies naive taker rule).

round4_phase2_complete.interaction_with_phase1: Mark 22 aggressive sell on VEV_5300 negative fwd_5 from Phase 1 is **refined** in Phase 2: same sign in tight spread bucket (mean -0.19, n=162) but **near-burst** window adds heterogeneous pair effects (e.g. hydro 14↔38 ± fwd near bursts). Mark 67→22/49 small-n positive forwards near bursts **echo** Phase 1 Mark 67 extract lift but are not standalone tradeable (n≤7). Phase 1 Mark 55 buy extract positive — Phase 2 extract flow lag OLS is **all zeros** (1366 rows), so signed-flow-on-same-timestamp does **not** replicate forward signal — use Phase 1 aggression merge instead.

bullets_phase2_plan.conclusion: Within ±300 of Mark01→22 multi-VEV bursts, dominant rows are still Mark01→22 on wings (fwd~0 on 6000/6500); hydro 14↔38 shows larger |mean| but tiny n (40s). No clean mean-revert vs trend split without holdout.
corr(Δspread, |fwd_5|) weak negative on extract/hydro (~-0.05), ~0 on VEV_5300 — no strong compression→vol edge at 5-row horizon.
Same-timestamp stacked flow lags regress to zero coefficients vs extract fwd_5 — this formulation does not recover Phase 1 participant conditioning.
Mark 22 on 5300: tight spread (≤2) bucket n=162 mean fwd_5 -0.19; wider bucket n=1 only — regime table sparse for wide book.
Not recomputed in this pass (no BS IV pipeline wired for R4 in repo scope this iteration).
Naive taker fade on every Mark22 print on 5300 yields **+1040** total worse=all but **-5002** days 1–2 vs **+6042** day 3 — **not day-stable**; do not ship as-is.
Skipped (no MM skew in trader_v1; needs position + counterparty joint model).

top5_tradeable_edges.edge: Conditional tables only: Mark 22 on VEV_5300 in tight spread — negative mean fwd_5 (tape)
top5_tradeable_edges.orthogonal_note: Same leg as Phase 1 rank-3 but adds spread bucket file
top5_tradeable_edges.sim_note: trader_v1 falsifies naive always-fade taker implementation

top5_tradeable_edges.edge: Near-burst hydro 14↔38 forward means spike |m5|>1 (n~40) — **hypothesis** for basket stress, not validated OOS
Burst calendar centers for Mark01→22 (structural count) — scheduling reference
Microprice field available for future microprice–mid divergence signals (computed in px pipeline)
Spread-change vs |fwd_5| weakly negative on extract — **null-tier** for directional use

round4_phase2_complete.phase3_prereq: Phase 2 gate satisfied for proceed to Sonic joint gate on R4 (Phase 3).

analysis_description: Round 4 Phase 3 (Sonic joint gate TH=2 on BBO ask1-bid1 for VEV_5200 & VEV_5300, inner join on timestamp per day — same convention as round3work/vouchers_final_strategy/analyze_vev_5200_5300_tight_gate_r3.py). Outputs: long panel, by-day forward extract K=20 tight vs loose, pooled spread–spread correlation matrix, counterparty×gate JSON (Mark67 extract, Mark22 5300), Mark01→22 on 5300 by day×tight. inclineGod: spread vs Δmid5200/5300 correlations in by_day CSV.
used_to_decide: round4_phase3_complete object below; trader_v2 gated sim vs v1.

sonic_joint_gate_on_r4.method: Per day: inner join VEV_5200, VEV_5300, VELVETFRUIT_EXTRACT on timestamp; spread=ask1-bid1; tight=(s5200<=2)&(s5300<=2); fwd_ext_k20 = m_ext.shift(-20)-m_ext
sonic_joint_gate_on_r4.comparison_to_phase1_2_without_gate: Phase 1 pooled extract forward after Mark67 aggr buy was positive (n~164). Under joint gate, **tight** Mark67 aggr-buy extract fwd5 mean **1.78** (n=27) vs **loose** **1.99** (n=137) — **gate does not sharpen** this counterparty edge (slightly lower mean in tight, small-n tight). Mark22 aggr sell on VEV_5300: **all 160** events occur when **tight** (loose n=0) — structural coupling of that print type to tight books. Extract **mid** forward K=20: **tight** means **+0.60/+0.46/+0.19** days 1–3 vs **loose** **-0.05/-0.02/-0.40** — **confirms Sonic story for extract mid** on R4 tape (especially days 1–2).
sonic_joint_gate_on_r4.spread_spread_inclineGod: Pooled corr matrix in r4_phase3_spread_spread_corr_pooled.csv; by-day corr(s5200,s_ext) and (s5300,s_ext) weak (see r4_phase3_gate_panel_by_day.csv). corr(s5200,dmid5200) and (s5300,dmid5300) small positive (~0.03–0.06) — spread co-moves weakly with tick mid step on gate legs.

counterparty_x_gate.Mark01_Mark22_VEV5300_tight: r4_phase3_mark01_to_22_on_5300_by_day_tight.csv — mean fwd5 negative on each day when tight (n 32/31/69); interaction is **sell pressure + tight book** still short-horizon down on 5300 mid.
counterparty_x_gate.Tier_A_sim: trader_v2 (joint gate AND Mark22 sell on 5300, taker fade) total **-3545** worse=all vs trader_v1 **+1040** without gate — **gating does not rescue** naive execution; day1 -3478, day2 -3749, day3 +3682 (still not stable).

ranked_edges.edge: Extract mid forward K=20 is substantially higher when Sonic joint tight vs loose on R4 (see gate_panel_by_day)
Mark 22 aggressive sells on VEV_5300 exist only under joint tight (n_loose=0)
Mark 01→Mark 22 on VEV_5300 under tight: negative mean fwd5 by day (consistent short-horizon pressure)
Three-way sim (gate+Mark22 taker) — negative total PnL; falsifies this execution rule

round4_phase3_complete.does_gate_clean_signal: Yes for **extract mid** forward (tight >> loose on mean fwd K=20 across days). **No** for sharpening **Mark 67** aggressive-buy forward (tight mean slightly below loose). **Mark 22** on 5300 is **only** observable under tight, so Phase 1 statistic is inherently gated — not an independent cross-check.
